title: Workfare Skills Support (Level-Up)
text:
Workfare Skills Support (Level-Up) - SupportGoWhere
Workfare Skills Support (Level-Up)
Share link
Enhanced tier of Workfare Skills Support (WSS), supporting lower-wage Singaporean workers who go for long-form training.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Provides training allowance of at least $300 per month
Covers a wide catalogue of long-form courses (including Nitec, Higher Nitec, Diplomas, Post-Diplomas, and Undergraduate courses)
Covers multiple bouts of long-form training
Apply online via the Training Allowance System using Singpass
Collapse all sections
About this support

Training Allowance (TA)   

Full-time training: 50% of average income over the latest available 12-month period, with minimum allowance of $300 per month 

Part-time training: $300 per month 




Coverage for multiple rounds of long-form training

Every individual can receive: 

Up to 24 months of monthly TA between ages 30 to 39

Up to another 24 months of monthly TA from age 40. This is a shared cap between WSS (Level-Up) and the SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme

For each age period, the cap is shared between both full-time and part-time training




Courses supported include:

SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme courses

Nitec, Higher Nitec, Diplomas, Post-Diplomas, and Undergraduate courses by Institutes of Higher Learning

From 2026 Q4, long-form Workforce Skills Qualifications (WSQ) full qualifications

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizen;

At least 30 years old or older as of 31 December of the work year;

Earn an average gross monthly income of not more than $3,000 for the past 12 months;

Have declared your net trade income and made MediSave contributions in full if you are self-employed;

Live in a property with an annual value of $21,000 and below as at 31 December of the preceding year;

Own not more than one property; and

If you are married:

you and your spouse together not own more than one property; and

the assessable income of your spouse does not exceed $70,000 of the preceding Year of Assessment




The above is similar to the Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) criteria.

How to apply?

You can apply online via the Training Allowance System (TAS) using your Singpass.

Where can I find help?

View more details

WSG Hotline: 1800 536 8333

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 19 May 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
